Effective Date of 1988 AmendmentAmendment by section 1013(a)(4)(A), (B)(i), (ii), (C), (5) of effective, except as otherwise provided, as if included in the provision of the Tax Reform Act of 1986, , to which such amendment relates, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 100–647Pub. L. 99–514section 1019(a) of Pub. L. 100–647section 1 of this title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title VI, § 6176(b)102 Stat. 3726Nov. 10, 1988“(1).—In generalThe amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to bonds issued after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 10, 1988“(2).—RefundingsThe amendment made by subsection (a) shall not apply to any bond issued to refund (or which is part of a series of bonds issued to refund) a bond issued on or before the date of the enactment of this Act if—“(A)the average maturity date of the issue of which the refunding bond is a part is not later than the average maturity date of the bonds to be refunded by such issue, and“(B)the amount of the refunding bond does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded bond.For purposes of subparagraph (A), average maturity shall be determined in accordance with section 147(b) of the 1986 Code.”

Application of Subsection (a)(12)(A)(ii)(I) to Refunding Bonds Issued BeforeJuly 1, 1987, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title I, § 1013(a)(4)(B)(iii)102 Stat. 3538Nov. 10, 1988“A refunding bond issued before , shall be treated as meeting the requirement of subclause (I) of section 144(a)(12)(A)(ii) of the 1986 Code if such bond met the requirement of such subclause as in effect before the amendments made by this subparagraph [amending this section].”July 1, 1987

Termination Date for Exemption for Certain Small Issues Under Section 103(b)(6), , , provided that:Pub. children.label: A
children.text: for the acquisition, construction, reconstruction, or improvement of land or property of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation, or

children.label: B
children.text: to redeem part or all of a prior issue which was issued for purposes described in subparagraph (A) or this subparagraph.

children.heading: Certain prior issues taken into account
children.label: 2 Certain prior issues taken into account
A
children.text: the proceeds of 2 or more issues of bonds (whether or not the issuer of each such issue is the same) are or will be used primarily with respect to facilities located in the same incorporated municipality or located in the same county (but not in any incorporated municipality),

children.label: B
children.text: the principal user of such facilities is or will be the same person or 2 or more related persons, and

children.label: C
children.text: but for this paragraph, paragraph (1) (or the corresponding provision of prior law) would apply to each such issue,

children.label: A
children.text: the relationship between such persons would result in a disallowance of losses under section 267 or 707(b), or

children.label: B
children.text: such persons are members of the same controlled group of corporations (as defined in section 1563(a), except that “more than 50 percent” shall be substituted for “at least 80 percent” each place it appears therein).

children.label: i
children.text: by substituting “$10,000,000” for “$1,000,000” in paragraph (1), and

children.label: ii
children.text: in determining the aggregate face amount of such issue, by taking into account not only the amount described in paragraph (2), but also the aggregate amount of capital expenditures with respect to facilities described in subparagraph (B) paid or incurred during the 6-year period beginning 3 years before the date of such issue and ending 3 years after such date (and financed otherwise than out of the proceeds of outstanding tax-exempt issues to which paragraph (1) (or the corresponding provision of prior law) applied), as if the aggregate amount of such capital expenditures constituted the face amount of a prior outstanding issue described in paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: located in the same incorporated municipality or located in the same county (but not in any incorporated municipality), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the principal user of which is or will be the same person or 2 or more related persons.

children.heading: Certain capital expenditures not taken into account
children.label: C Certain capital expenditures not taken into account
i
children.text: to replace property destroyed or damaged by fire, storm, or other casualty, to the extent of the fair market value of the property replaced,

children.label: ii
children.text: required by a change made after the date of issue of the issue in question in a Federal or State law or local ordinance of general application or required by a change made after such date in rules and regulations of general application issued under such a law or ordinance,

children.label: iii
children.text: required by circumstances which could not be reasonably foreseen on such date of issue or arising out of a mistake of law or fact (but the aggregate amount of expenditures not taken into account under this clause with respect to any issue shall not exceed $1,000,000), or

children.label: iv
children.text: described in clause (i) or (ii) of section 41(b)(2)(A) for which a deduction was allowed under section 174A(a),

children.heading: Limitation on loss of tax exemption
children.label: D Limitation on loss of tax exemption
children.text: In applying subparagraph (A)(ii) with respect to capital expenditures made after the date of any issue, no bond issued as a part of such issue shall cease to be treated as a qualified small issue bond by reason of any such expenditure for any period before the date on which such expenditure is paid or incurred.

children.heading: Certain refinancing issues
children.label: E Certain refinancing issues
children.text: In the case of any issue described in paragraph (1)(B), an election may be made under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph only if all of the prior issues being redeemed are issues to which paragraph (1) (or the corresponding provision of prior law) applied. In applying subparagraph (A)(ii) with respect to such a refinancing issue, capital expenditures shall be taken into account only for purposes of determining whether the prior issues being redeemed qualified (and would have continued to qualify) under paragraph (1) (or the corresponding provision of prior law).

children.heading: Aggregate amount of capital expenditures where there is urban development action grant
children.label: F Aggregate amount of capital expenditures where there is urban development action grant
children.text: In the case of any issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used to provide facilities with respect to which an urban development action grant has been made under section 119 of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974, capital expenditures of not to exceed $10,000,000 shall not be taken into account for purposes of applying subparagraph (A)(ii). This subparagraph shall not apply to bonds issued after .December 31, 2006

children.heading: Additional capital expenditures not taken into account
children.label: G Additional capital expenditures not taken into account
children.text: With respect to bonds issued after , in addition to any capital expenditure described in subparagraph (C), capital expenditures of not to exceed $10,000,000 shall not be taken into account for purposes of applying subparagraph (A)(ii).December 31, 2006

children.heading: Issues for residential purposes
children.label: 5 Issues for residential purposes
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any bond issued as part of an issue 5 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used directly or indirectly to provide residential real property for family units.

children.heading: Limitations on treatment of bonds as part of the same issue
children.label: 6 Limitations on treatment of bonds as part of the same issue
i
children.text: which are located in more than 1 State, or

children.label: ii
children.text: which have, or will have, as the same principal user the same person or related persons.

children.label: i
children.text: guarantees, arranges, participates in, or assists with the issuance (or pays any portion of the cost of issuance) of any bond the proceeds of which are to be used to finance or refinance such facility, and

children.label: ii
children.text: provides any property, or any franchise, trademark, or trade name (within the meaning of section 1253), which is to be used in connection with such facility.

children.heading: Subsection not to apply if bonds issued with certain other tax-exempt bonds
children.label: 7 Subsection not to apply if bonds issued with certain other tax-exempt bonds
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any bond issued as part of an issue (other than an issue to which paragraph (4) applies) if the interest on any other bond which is part of such issue is excluded from gross income under any provision of law other than this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: more than 25 percent of the net proceeds of the issue are to be used to provide a facility the primary purpose of which is one of the following: retail food and beverage services, automobile sales or service, or the provision of recreation or entertainment; or

children.label: B
children.text: any portion of the proceeds of the issue is to be used to provide the following: any private or commercial golf course, country club, massage parlor, tennis club, skating facility (including roller skating, skateboard, and ice skating), racquet sports facility (including any handball or racquetball court), hot tub facility, suntan facility, or racetrack.

children.heading: Aggregation of issues with respect to single project
children.label: 9 Aggregation of issues with respect to single project
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, 2 or more issues part or all of the net proceeds of which are to be used with respect to a single building, an enclosed shopping mall, or a strip of offices, stores, or warehouses using substantial common facilities shall be treated as 1 issue (and any person who is a principal user with respect to any of such issues shall be treated as a principal user with respect to the aggregated issue).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any issue if the aggregate authorized face amount of such issue allocated to any test-period beneficiary (when increased by the outstanding tax-exempt facility-related bonds of such beneficiary) exceeds $40,000,000.

children.label: I
children.text: which are allocated to such beneficiary, and

children.label: II
children.text: which are outstanding at the time of such later issue (not including as outstanding any bond which is to be redeemed (other than in an advance refunding) from the net proceeds of the later issue).

children.label: I
children.text: exempt facility bonds, qualified small issue bonds, and qualified redevelopment bonds, and

children.label: II
children.text: industrial development bonds (as defined in section 103(b)(2), as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1986) to which section 141(a) does not apply.

children.heading: Allocation of face amount of issue
children.label: C Allocation of face amount of issue

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in regulations, the portion of the face amount of an issue allocated to any test-period beneficiary of a facility financed by the proceeds of such issue (other than an owner of such facility) is an amount which bears the same relationship to the entire face amount of such issue as the portion of such facility used by such beneficiary bears to the entire facility.

children.heading: Owners
children.label: ii Owners
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in regulations, the portion of the face amount of an issue allocated to any test-period beneficiary who is an owner of a facility financed by the proceeds of such issue is an amount which bears the same relationship to the entire face amount of such issue as the portion of such facility owned by such beneficiary bears to the entire facility.

children.label: i
children.text: the date such facilities were placed in service, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of issue.

children.heading: Treatment of related persons
children.label: E Treatment of related persons
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, all persons who are related (within the meaning of paragraph (3)) to each other shall be treated as 1 person.

children.heading: Limitation on acquisition of depreciable farm property
children.label: 11 Limitation on acquisition of depreciable farm property

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any issue if more than $250,000 of the net proceeds of such issue are to be used to provide depreciable farm property with respect to which the principal user is or will be the same person or 2 or more related persons.

children.heading: Depreciable farm property
children.label: B Depreciable farm property
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “depreciable farm property” means property of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation which is to be used in a trade or business of farming.

children.heading: Prior issues taken into account
children.label: C Prior issues taken into account
children.text: In determining the amount of proceeds of an issue to be used as described in subparagraph (A), there shall be taken into account the aggregate amount of each prior issue to which paragraph (1) (or the corresponding provisions of prior law) applied which were or will be so used.

children.label: i
children.text: any bond (other than a bond described in clause (ii)) issued after , orDecember 31, 1986

children.label: I
children.text: the average maturity date of the issue of which the refunding bond is a part is not later than the average maturity date of the bonds to be refunded by such issue,

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of the refunding bond does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded bond, and

children.label: III
children.text: the net proceeds of the refunding bond are used to redeem the refunded bond not later than 90 days after the date of the issuance of the refunding bond.

children.heading: Bonds issued to finance manufacturing facilities and farm property
children.label: B Bonds issued to finance manufacturing facilities and farm property
i
children.text: any manufacturing facility, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any land or property in accordance with section 147(c)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “manufacturing facility” means any facility which is used in the manufacturing or production of tangible personal property (including the processing resulting in a change in the condition of such property). A rule similar to the rule of section 142(b)(2) shall apply for purposes of the preceding sentence.

children.label: I
children.text: such facilities are located on the same site as the manufacturing facility, and

children.label: II
children.text: not more than 25 percent of the net proceeds of the issue are used to provide such facilities.

children.heading: Special rules for bonds issued in 2009 and 2010
children.label: iii Special rules for bonds issued in 2009 and 2010
I
children.text: a facility which is used in the creation or production of intangible property which is described in section 197(d)(1)(C)(iii), or

children.label: II
children.text: a facility which is functionally related and subordinate to a manufacturing facility (determined without regard to this subclause) if such facility is located on the same site as the manufacturing facility.

children.label: I
children.text: the maximum amount of loans outstanding to any student, and

children.label: II
children.text: the maximum rate of interest payable on any loan,

children.label: ii
children.text: the loans are directly or indirectly guaranteed by the Federal Government,

children.label: iii
children.text: the financing of loans under the program is not limited by Federal law to the proceeds of tax-exempt bonds, and

children.label: I
children.text: are authorized to be paid with respect to loans made under the program, or

children.label: II
children.text: would be authorized to be made with respect to loans under the program if such loans were not financed with the proceeds of tax-exempt bonds, or

children.label: B
children.text: a program of general application approved by the State if no loan under such program exceeds the difference between the total cost of attendance and other forms of student assistance (not including loans pursuant to section 428B(a)(1) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (relating to parent loans) or subpart I of part C of title VII of the Public Health Service Act (relating to student assistance)) for which the student borrower may be eligible. A program shall not be treated as described in this subparagraph if such program is described in subparagraph (A).1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: 90 percent in the case of the program described in paragraph (1)(A), and

children.label: B
children.text: 95 percent in the case of the program described in paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Student borrowers must be residents of issuing State, etc.
children.label: 3 Student borrowers must be residents of issuing State, etc.
A
children.text: a resident of the State from which the volume cap under section 146 for such loan was derived, or

children.label: B
children.text: enrolled at an educational institution located in such State.

children.heading: Discrimination on basis of school location not permitted
children.label: 4 Discrimination on basis of school location not permitted
children.text: A program shall not be treated as described in paragraph (1)(A) if such program discriminates on the basis of the location (in the United States) of the educational institution in which the student is enrolled.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified redevelopment bond” means any bond issued as part of an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used for 1 or more redevelopment purposes in any designated blighted area.

children.label: i
children.text: a State law which authorizes the issuance of such bonds for redevelopment purposes in blighted areas, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a redevelopment plan which is adopted before such issuance by the governing body described in paragraph (4)(A) with respect to the designated blighted area,

children.label: i
children.text: the payment of the principal and interest on such issue is primarily secured by taxes of general applicability imposed by a general purpose governmental unit, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any increase in real property tax revenues (attributable to increases in assessed value) by reason of the carrying out of such purposes in such area is reserved exclusively for debt service on such issue (and similar issues) to the extent such increase does not exceed such debt service,

children.label: i
children.text: which is acquired by a governmental unit with the proceeds of the issue, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which is transferred to a person other than a governmental unit,

children.label: D
children.text: the financed area with respect to such issue meets the no additional charge requirements of paragraph (5), and

children.label: E
children.text: the use of the proceeds of the issue meets the requirements of paragraph (6).

children.label: i
children.text: the acquisition (by a governmental unit having the power to exercise eminent domain) of real property located in such area,

children.label: ii
children.text: the clearing and preparation for redevelopment of land in such area which was acquired by such governmental unit,

children.label: iii
children.text: the rehabilitation of real property located in such area which was acquired by such governmental unit, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the relocation of occupants of such real property.

children.heading: New construction not permitted
children.label: B New construction not permitted
children.text: The term “redevelopment purposes” does not include the construction (other than the rehabilitation) of any property or the enlargement of an existing building.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “designated blighted area” means any blighted area designated by the governing body of a local general purpose governmental unit in the jurisdiction of which such area is located.

children.heading: Blighted area
children.label: B Blighted area
children.text: The term “blighted area” means any area which the governing body described in subparagraph (A) determines to be a blighted area on the basis of the substantial presence of factors such as excessive vacant land on which structures were previously located, abandoned or vacant buildings, substandard structures, vacancies, and delinquencies in payment of real property taxes.

children.heading: Designated areas may not exceed 20 percent of total assessed value of real property in government’s jurisdiction
children.label: C Designated areas may not exceed 20 percent of total assessed value of real property in government’s jurisdiction

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An area may be designated by a governmental unit as a blighted area only if the designation percentage with respect to such area, when added to the designation percentages of all other designated blighted areas within the jurisdiction of such governmental unit, does not exceed 20 percent.

children.heading: Designation percentage
children.label: ii Designation percentage
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “designation percentage” means, with respect to any area, the percentage (determined at the time such area is designated) which the assessed value of real property located in such area is of the total assessed value of all real property located within the jurisdiction of the governmental unit which designated such area.

children.heading: Exception where bonds not outstanding
children.label: iii Exception where bonds not outstanding
children.text: The designation percentage of a previously designated blighted area shall not be taken into account under clause (i) if no qualified redevelopment bond (or similar bond) is or will be outstanding with respect to such area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an area shall not be treated as a designated blighted area for purposes of this subsection unless such area is contiguous and compact and its area equals or exceeds 100 acres.

children.heading: 10-acre minimum in certain cases
children.label: ii 10-acre minimum in certain cases
children.text: Clause (i) shall be applied by substituting “10 acres” for “100 acres” if not more than 25 percent of the financed area is to be provided (pursuant to the issue and all other such issues) to 1 person. For purposes of the preceding sentence, all related persons (as defined in subsection (a)(3)) shall be treated as 1 person. For purposes of this clause, an area provided to a developer on a short-term interim basis shall not be treated as provided to such developer.

children.label: A
children.text: no owner or user of property located in the financed area is subject to a charge or fee which similarly situated owners or users of comparable property located outside such area are not subject, and

children.label: B
children.text: the assessment method or rate of real property taxes with respect to property located in the financed area does not differ from the assessment method or rate of real property taxes with respect to comparable property located outside such area.

children.label: A
children.text: not more than 25 percent of the net proceeds of such issue are to be used to provide (including the provision of land for) facilities described in subsection (a)(8) or section 147(e), and

children.label: B
children.text: no portion of the proceeds of such issue is to be used to provide (including the provision of land for) any private or commercial golf course, country club, massage parlor, hot tub facility, suntan facility, racetrack or other facility used for gambling, or any store the principal business of which is the sale of alcoholic beverages for consumption off premises.

children.heading: Financed area
children.label: 7 Financed area
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “financed area” means, with respect to any issue, the portion of the designated blighted area with respect to which the proceeds of such issue are to be used.

children.heading: Restriction on acquisition of land not to apply
children.label: 8 Restriction on acquisition of land not to apply
children.text: Section 147(c) (other than paragraphs (1)(B) and (2) thereof) shall not apply to any qualified redevelopment bond.
